purpose: Measure description-triggering accuracy: does the skill fire on in-scope visualization requests and correctly defer to sibling skills otherwise? Run with the skill-creator eval harness (ai-evaluation-harness).

should_trigger.prompt: Draw me a network topology diagram of our Azure estate, hub-and-spoke, with the risky subnets coloured.
Generate a Confluence-ready draw.io diagram of all our VNets and peerings across subscriptions.
I need an HLD and MLD view of the network that refreshes itself and shows exposure findings on the map.
Visualize which resources are internet-exposed across the management group as a coloured diagram.
Our generated topology diagram has no peering edges and everything is green — fix the visualization.
should_trigger.why: core: the exact RC-1..RC-4 failure this skill prevents

should_trigger.prompt: Make a multi-hub network map with the internet/ExpressRoute boundary drawn.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Review our Azure network for reachability and segmentation risk and give me a prioritized findings report.
Write a VP-ready escalation memo about the network exposure we found.
Generate the Terraform for a new hub-and-spoke topology from this intent.
Forecast the cost of adding an Azure Firewall and a second spoke.
Build the Go MCP server that exposes get_topology and analyze_risks.
Diagram our AWS VPC peering and security groups.
should_not_trigger.why: non-Azure: skill must decline and say so

scoring.trigger_recall: fraction of should_trigger prompts that route to this skill (target >= 0.95)
scoring.trigger_precision: 1 - (fraction of should_not_trigger prompts that wrongly route here) (target = 1.0; false-positives are worse than misses here)

notes: The hard boundary is map (this skill) vs report (analysis/reviewer). Most confusions will be should_not_trigger #1/#2 leaking in; weight those in review.
